wordmark.purpose: The Crest brand mark only - sidebar and login screen. Never reused for a heading.

body.purpose: Default for every table cell, form value, and paragraph of UI copy.

label.purpose: Table column headers, stat card labels, form field labels - the uppercase-tracked tier.

shadows.name: floating-action
shadows.purpose: The one static elevation shadow, for content fixed above the page (e.g. a bottom-anchored cart summary button).

shadows.name: live-pulse-ring
shadows.purpose: Breathing ring animation on elements needing real-time attention (new order banner, live badge). Uses the relevant signal color's own alpha.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: The one formalized easing curve, used for sidebar nav hover/active transitions.

motion.name: motion-fast
motion.purpose: Duration paired with ease-standard for sidebar interactions.

motion.name: button-transition
motion.purpose: Default button/tab/badge state-change speed. No bounce, no elastic, no transform-on-press.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single call-to-action button style. Accent background, theme-paired foreground, 700 weight.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; padding: 8px 16px; border-radius: 6px; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 700; cursor: pointer; border: none; background: var(--theme-accent); color: var(--theme-accent-text); transition: background 0.13s, color 0.13s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: var(--theme-accent-hover); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--theme-focus-ring); }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: The default secondary/tertiary action button - quiet until hovered.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; padding: 8px 16px; border-radius: 6px; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; background: var(--theme-input-bg); color: var(--theme-text1); border: 1px solid var(--theme-border-lt); transition: background 0.13s, color 0.13s, border-color 0.13s; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: var(--theme-table-hover); border-color: var(--theme-accent); }

components.name: Status Badge
components.description: Alpha-tinted status chip. Shown here in the success variant; danger/warning/gray follow the same pattern with their own signal color.
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-block; padding: 2px 8px; border-radius: 4px; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 500; text-transform: capitalize; } .ds-badge-green { background: rgba(52,211,153,0.10); color: var(--theme-green); }

components.name: Card
components.description: The base surface for grouped content - flat, bordered, one tone lighter than the page.
components.css: .ds-card { background: var(--theme-card); border: 1px solid var(--theme-border); border-radius: 10px; padding: 24px; } .ds-card-title { margin: 0 0 8px; font-size: 11px; letter-spacing: 0.1em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--theme-text3); } .ds-card-value { margin: 0; font-size: 15px; font-weight: 700; color: var(--theme-text1); }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Labeled form field with the theme-colored focus ring - label always sits above, never a placeholder standing in for it.
components.css: .ds-field { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 6px; } .ds-field-label { font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; color: var(--theme-text2); letter-spacing: 0.04em; } .ds-field-input { background: var(--theme-input-bg); border: 1px solid var(--theme-border); border-radius: 6px; padding: 9px 12px; font-size: 13px; color: var(--theme-text1); outline: none; transition: border-color 0.14s; } .ds-field-input:focus { border-color: var(--theme-accent); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--theme-focus-ring); } .ds-field-input::placeholder { color: var(--theme-text3); }

narrative.overview: Crest Inventory is operated, not visited. An owner is checking margin between service rushes; an accountant is reconciling TDS figures before a filing deadline. Neither has time for the interface to perform. The system is built around legibility under pressure: dense tables that stay scannable, a single restrained accent, and status communicated through color and position rather than decoration. This deliberately rejects two things named in PRODUCT.md: the dated, hierarchy-less density of legacy Nepali ERP software, and the templated purple-gradient look of generic AI-generated SaaS. The product runs across nine interchangeable theme presets, all built from the same token set; every rule is written against the default preset's values, but the relationships between tokens hold across all nine.
keyCharacteristics: Flat by default: borders and tonal fills carry hierarchy, not shadows
One accent color per screen, applied sparingly, never as a wash
A serif wordmark is the one deliberate ornamental choice in an otherwise all-sans, all-functional system
Status is color-coded consistently, with a rationed 4th color reserved for genuine 4th/5th categories

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Aged Brass is the only non-semantic color on any screen. If a second brand color shows up anywhere outside the rationed purple exception, it's a mistake, not a design choice.

rules.name: The Accent-Text Pairing Rule
rules.body: Any element with an Aged-Brass background uses the theme's paired accent-text token for its foreground, never a hardcoded white or black - the accent color changes per theme preset and a hardcoded foreground will fail contrast on at least one of the nine.

rules.name: The One Serif Rule
rules.body: Georgia appears exactly once per screen (the wordmark, if visible at all). Never used for a heading, callout, or emphasis.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: A card, table, or panel gets a background shift and a border. It does not also get a shadow for polish. Shadow is a signal (floating, or needs-attention-now), not decoration.

dos: Do use the theme's accent-text token as the foreground on any accent-colored background
Do keep tonal alpha-tints at 2-18% opacity
Do wrap every wide table in the horizontal-scroll container
Do reserve the rationed 4th color (purple) for a genuine 4th/5th categorical need
Do put labels above form fields, always
donts: Don't build dense, hierarchy-less layouts in the name of fitting more in
Don't reach for purple gradients, Inter-everywhere, or a templated hero-plus-three-cards layout
Don't add box-shadow to a card or panel for polish
Don't hardcode white or black as text on an accent background
Don't use a second saturated brand color alongside Aged Brass on the same screen
